title: Uniface 10: Changes in Terminology
breadcrumbs: What's New in Uniface 10 Compared to Uniface 9
text: Uniface 10: Changes in Terminology Uniface 10 introduces a slightly different conceptual paradigm. Most existing concepts have been maintained, but some have been transformed or discarded, and several have been renamed. Search: Column Uniface 9 Term Uniface 10 Term Remarks Filter results... 21 rows found Table 1. Terminology Differences Between Uniface 9 and Uniface 10 Uniface 9 Term Uniface 10 Term Remarks Filter 21 rows found Filter 21 rows found Filter 21 rows found Uniface 9 Term Uniface 10 Term Remarks application model modeled objects The application model no longer exists as a separate object. The entities that made up the application model are now called modeled entities and each entity name has a suffix that identifies a model namespace. The term modeled objects encompasses not only modeled entities, but also modeled components and modeled properties (formerly component templates and various property templates). — system triggers System triggers are script modules that are fired in response to system commands, such as validate and retrieve , and not directly by the user. — interactive triggers Interactive triggers are script modules that are associated with object representations in the user interface, which can be a widget trigger like OnEdit, but also a field-level trigger such as getFocus. The are executed in response to a user event, such as clicking a button or editing a field. Interactive triggers include the (former) extended triggers, web triggers, as well as form-specific triggers that react to user actions. extended triggers interactive triggers Uniface 9 extended triggers were widget-specific, usually on the field-level widget, and declared using the trigger keyword in a single code container. In Uniface 10, these triggers fall under the term interactive triggers . standard triggers — In Uniface 9, the term standard triggers was applied to triggers that were implicitly declared by a named code container. Some triggers (Operations, Local Proc Modules, Extended Triggers) were not triggers at all, but simply code containers. In Uniface 10, all ProcScript modules are explicitly declared, and a more useful distinction between interactive and system triggers has been applied. Proc Global Proc ProcScript Global ProcScript Script is used to write procedural logic. Depending on where this logic needs to be executed, script is either ProcScript (server) or JavaScript (browser). However, even JavaScript must first be declared as such in a web trigger or web operation ProcScript module. Include Proc IncludeScript In Uniface 10, the term has been changed to reflect that code that is included during compilation may be ProcScript or JavaScript. IncludeScript is no longer considered to be a global object. Proc listing script listing Script modules can contain JavaScript, so the term Proc listing has been changed to script listing , or just listing . global objects global objects Global objects are resolved at runtime, whereas IncludeScript is resolved during compilation, so it does not belong in the list of global objects. This distinction is now easier because IncludeScripts are now created and maintained in a separate typed library. template modeled object In Uniface 9, the term templates was applied to a mixture of copied and inherited objects. In the Uniface 10, the terms have been changed to make this distinction clear: An object that is reused to create a new object is a modeled object, and it is inherited by the newly-created object An object that is copied to create a new object is a template — derived object Modeled objects that are re-used by other objects are said to be derived from the modeled object. Thus a component entity that reuses and inherits from a modeled entity is a derived entity. component template modeled component Component templates are now know as modeled components, and can be created in the same way as other components. attached component derived component attached component In Uniface 9, the term attached component was dependent on context. In Uniface 10, the ambiguity is removed: A component that is derived from a modeled component is a derived component. A component that is started in attached mode is an attached component. entity interface template field interface template field layout template field syntax template modeled entity interface modeled field Interface modeled field layout modeled field syntax The Uniface 9 property templates can be inherited, so they are now called modeled properties. Uniface Development Environment UDE Uniface IDE Integrated Development Environment (IDE) is the standard term used for software development tools. DOL and URR — Classic deployment is no longer supported, so all library objects are delivered in Uniface archive files, and no longer in .dol and .urr files. ULANA and USYSANA — These tables were required for classic deployment, which is not supported in Uniface 10. boilerplate field static Field At runtime, the content of these fields is actually shared by all occurrences of the entity because they are bound to the entity rather than to the occurrence. The term static field is preferred to boilerplate field . You create a static field by setting the property Is Static? to True . widget properties window properties representation properties widget properties Any object that can be represented in the presentation layer, including the application shell, component, component entity, and occurrence, may have an associated widget, even if it is hard-coded and implicit. For example, the window is the widget of a form or application shell, and the default widget for an entity in a form is a nameless widget. The properties of these objects are handled in the same way, so the term widget properties now encompasses them. For more information, see Widgets and Properties for Styling the User Interface . library system library component library application library Library typed library Libraries contain sets of common shared resources. In Uniface 10, libraries are typed , so you have separate libraries for messages, Global ProcScripts, menus and so on. However, libraries of different types can have the same name. For more information, see Libraries . classic deployment — Classic deployment mode is no longer supported. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
